data.narrative:
Biofire is a Series A–stage defense and security company that has raised $52.9M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 10 peer companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including same-stage peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis used total capital raised ($53M) as a proxy base and applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $106M. Peer-median analysis examined four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $225M and a range spanning $125M to $500M. This peer cohort directly anchors market-observed pricing for similarly-positioned defense tech companies.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $90M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $165M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer valuations—notably the 4x gap between the lowest and highest comparable—and uncertainty around Biofire's relative positioning within the defense tech sector on dimensions including revenue traction, technical differentiation, and customer validation. The peer-median method ($225M) pulls meaningfully higher than stage-progression ($106M), suggesting either elevated investor appetite in current defense tech markets or that Biofire's profile warrants premium positioning relative to pure capital-raised metrics.
